changeSet.author: Ivica Cardic
changeSet.comment: Dedup workspace_user on (workspace_id, user_id) before adding the unique constraint. Preserves the highest-privilege row (MIN(workspace_role) \u2014 0=ADMIN is the most privileged ordinal); ties broken by MIN(id) so the longest-surviving row wins when roles are identical. Without this preference, a duplicate history like {id=1 EDITOR, id=2 ADMIN} would silently demote the ADMIN on migration (the MIN(id) row would win), with no audit trail or warning. Idempotent: on a database with no duplicates this is a no-op.
changeSet.sql: DELETE FROM workspace_user wu USING workspace_user wu2 WHERE wu.workspace_id = wu2.workspace_id AND wu.user_id = wu2.user_id AND ( wu.workspace_role > wu2.workspace_role OR (wu.workspace_role = wu2.workspace_role AND wu.id > wu2.id) )
changeSet.comment: Add unique constraint on workspace_user(workspace_id, user_id)
